message: My 8-year-old asked me last week why our smoke alarm was beeping. I realized I'd been meaning to change the battery for... weeks. So I grabbed her, showed her how to test it, changed the battery together, and talked through what that sound actually *means*. She went from "annoying noise" to "that's my friend telling us something." Then she wanted to know what we'd do if there was a real fire. Turns out, kids take this stuff seriously when you make it real instead of scary—she even drew a map of our escape route and taped it to her wall. I think having her involved actually made *me* take it more seriously too. Do your kids know what to do, or have they never really thought about it?
